source: Extracted via IDA step-5 script from AOCClient-Win64-Shipping.exe
_notes: cmd_index order = declaration order of Net-flagged properties across the
full inheritance chain (UObject -> AActor -> AController -> APlayerController
-> AAoCPlayerController).  UE5's RepLayout walks the chain from topmost parent
to most derived and assigns cmd_indices sequentially to CPF_Net properties only.
Rollback semantic: per Function J, properties whose data write doesn't advance
the archive are rolled back (both cmd_index and data).  So the wire contains
TODO: AController and further APawn scans needed to fill gaps.  ReplicatedMovement
may actually be first in cmd_index order because UE5 places it near the top.

aactor_net_properties.name: AuthServerIDReplicated
aactor_net_properties.likely_type: uint32 or FGuid (AoC custom addition to AActor)
aactor_net_properties.note: This is AoC-specific — not in stock UE5 AActor.  Added by AoC for cross-server ID tracking.

aactor_notes: 8 Net properties found.  Stock UE5 AActor typically has MORE (e.g. AttachmentReplication).
The step-5 scan broke the AActor array into 3 separate regions due to non-plausible
entries in between.  May need to widen the struct-pointer plausibility range and rescan.
acontroller_notes: Step-6 scan using seeds 'bAttachToPawn' and 'ControlRotation' located the correct
AController table — 4 entries: Character, TransformComponent, ControlRotation, bAttachToPawn.
NONE have the Net flag!  In stock UE5, AController::PlayerState and AController::Pawn
are Net-replicated.  AoC has apparently REMOVED these base-class replications and moved
them to AAoCPlayerController as custom fields (ControllersOriginalPawn, ControlledExternalPawn).
This is a significant architectural divergence from stock UE5, consistent with AoC's
aplayercontroller_notes: Only 1 Net property (TargetViewRotation) in the 42-entry APlayerController table.
Stock UE5 confirms APlayerController itself adds very few Net properties — most
come from inherited AController/AActor.  PlayerCameraManager is NOT Net (it's

predicted_cmd_index_order.__gap_more_apc: ? possibly more if earlier scan was incomplete
